people_highlights.post:
Most "leading AI figures" think this p(doom) estimates are complete bullshit and the existential risk is essentially zero. 
But most of them are silent. 
The doomers attract a disproportionate amount of attention, of course.
people_highlights.engagement: Likes=615, Reposts=43, Quotes=10, Replies=57, Bookmarks=29, Views=22296
people_highlights.why: This tweet provides Yann LeCun's rebuttal to AI doomerism, asserting that most leading figures dismiss high p(doom) estimates, which is significant for understanding expert consensus on AI risks.
people_highlights.name: Yann LeCun

people_highlights.post: These neuroscience adjacent ideas are exotic armchair philosophy when the LLM simulation path will work really well and so much faster and it's not even remotely a contest.
people_highlights.why: This tweet emphasizes Andrej Karpathy's view that AI-driven LLM simulations offer a faster and more practical path to brain uploading compared to traditional neuroscience methods.
people_highlights.name: Andrej Karpathy

people_highlights.post: “They are smart … for humans” – future AI
people_highlights.why: This tweet reflects Elon Musk's perspective on the future of AI surpassing human intelligence while acknowledging human ingenuity in projects like Starship.
people_highlights.name: Elon Musk

people_highlights.post:
it’s not a vindication for classical AI. It’s a vindication for hybrids.
extensibility was core to LISP, the first central language of AI, designed by McCarthy who I mentioned.
people_highlights.why: This tweet underscores Gary Marcus's advocacy for hybrid AI systems, contributing to ongoing debates in AI development.
people_highlights.name: Gary Marcus

people_highlights.post: I'm getting better quality code because if I spot a tedious minor improvement - a slight readability tweak that needs me to update code in 20+ places - I tell the coding agent to do it, costs me nothing
people_highlights.why: This highlights how AI coding agents can enhance developer productivity by automating repetitive code refinements.
people_highlights.name: Simon Willison

people_highlights.post: (thinking traces are always summaries of the internal reasoning, since the actual internal chain of thought is a trade secret, but that doesn't mean they aren't useful when summarized, especially when those summaries contain the tools used by the model and web searches it made)
people_highlights.why: This tweet provides insight into how AI models handle and display their reasoning processes, which is crucial for transparency and effective use of AI tools.
people_highlights.name: Ethan Mollick

trending_posts.author: @Hesamation
trending_posts.name: ℏεsam
trending_posts.post:
AMD Senior AI Director confirms Claude has been nerfed. She analyzed Claude's session logs from Janurary to March:
> median thinking dropped from ~2,200 to ~600 chars
> API requests went up 80x from Feb to Mar. less thinking and failed attempts meaning more retries, burning more tokens, and spending more on tokens
> reads-per-edit dropped from 6.6x → 2.0x. model stops researching code before touching it.
> model tried to bail out or ask "should i continue" 173 times in 17 days (0 times before March 8).

Anthropic’s Claude Opus is FALLING.

Latest benchmarks show its accuracy dropped from 83.3% → 68.3% in just days.

That’s a major spike in hallucinations during coding.

Grok 4.20 still holds the #1 spot. Undefeated.

briefing.community_pulse:
• **Linux Community's Debate on AI-Generated Code Rules**: @Pirat_Nation highlighted the ongoing controversy in the Linux kernel community, where after months of debate, new guidelines allow tools like GitHub Copilot but reject "low-quality AI slop." The post quotes maintainers emphasizing human responsibility: “Humans take the fall for mistakes.” Backed by Linus Torvalds, this has sparked discussions on AI's role in open-source development[[1]](https://x.com/i/status/2043404282884395012).

• **Waymo Autonomous Vehicles: Jobs vs. Safety Controversy**: @AdamThierer criticized @MorePerfectUS for downplaying the safety benefits of AI-driven self-driving cars while defending taxi medallions. In response to a video claiming Waymo could displace 2 million workers and lower wages, Thierer called it "astonishing and shameful," linking to an essay on AI policy lessons from driverless cars[[2]](https://x.com/i/status/2043759883871309969).

• **Anyma & LISA's "Bad Angel" MV: AI vs. CGI Debate**: @TheePopCore reported on the controversy surrounding whether the music video used AI, with director Gianluigi Carella confirming it was all CGI and VFX: “Not aware this was a debate but yes an uncalculable amount of hours, sweat and blood was poured into this." This quelled debates in the pop music community about AI's involvement[[3]](https://x.com/i/status/2042462082319466789).

• **X Platform Account Suppression Possibly Involving AI**: @FischerKing64 sparked debate on content moderation, claiming "obvious suppression of some accounts" by either insiders or AI, making the platform "pointless" if users can't reach followers. This echoes broader controversies about AI-driven algorithms on social media[[4]](https://x.com/i/status/2043183666327871936).

• **Cultural Debate on AI Use in Discussions Among Indians**: In a thread, @dudnbfin questioned @Z0R0_000000 on why "indians love ai and never actually debate on their own," amid a conversation about history and aesthetics, highlighting tensions around AI's role in online debates and knowledge sharing[[5]](https://x.com/i/status/2043059711038181388).
